question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Summary - Based on the issues you described, three of the four problems match known incident patterns in our records: DevOps → TRAIN connection failures caused by ADFS client parameters, external guests losing sporadic SharePoint access caused by Azure AD Connect / duplicate-group synchronization, and desktop Outlook clients stuck in Outbox caused by SMTP AUTH being disabled by tenant security defaults

Could you please reopen this ticket? Thank you and best regards Employee 14 18.12.24 00:33 Ticket closed | TI-1385257 Dear Employee 14, Your ticket from 26.11.24 10:07 has been closed. Closure description: Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment. 18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA Dear Employee 7, During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true: EnableJWT RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails. RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing. The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims. We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved. Kind regards Employee 21 Notes: 26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16. 26.11.24 Ticket assigned

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - The mobile device-management “certificate bridge unhealthy” and the missing meeting-room remote are not clearly represented in the provided ticket data, so I cannot confidently diagnose those from the current dataset. Findings (mapped to your four items) 1) DevOps tool / TRAIN environment (background sign-in works in browser but not for service/account) - The dataset contains a matching incident where DevOps / FO connectivity to the TRAIN CE server failed for background/service logins while interactive browser logins worked. The root cause there was incorrectly set ADFS client parameters (EnableJWT and RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders) causing authentication failures; the ticket was resolved by adjusting the ADFS parameters to match the TEST environment

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . 2) External consultants losing SharePoint folder access intermittently - There is a directly comparable incident: external guests intermittently lost access to a project folder because Azure AD Connect synchronization overwrote/duplicated groups (a cloud-native group and a synchronized on prem group had the same name but different object IDs). SharePoint evaluates group membership by Object ID, so external guests (managed cloud-only) lost effective membership after sync; resolution required consolidating groups, cleaning up objects and running a full sync
context: SOURCE 13: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically Ticket number: TI-1583327 Company: Customer Company 25 AG Contact person: Contact Person 07 Owner: Employee 31 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 07.04.25 08:12 Closure Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18 Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable. Activities 07.04.25 08:04 Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint From: Contact Person 07 Good morning Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder /Projects/Compliance_Review_2025 Internal employees do not appear to have issues. Please review. Kind regards Contact Person 07 07.04.25 08:12 Ticket created Assigned to: Employee 31 Status: In Progress Notes 07.04.25 09:05 Helpdesk asks: • Does it affect only external users? • What error message appears? • Since when exactly? • Were groups or permissions adjusted? 07.04.25 10:20 Customer responds: Error message: Access denied One user could access yesterday. Today no longer. No deliberate changes made. 07.04.25 11:10 Helpdesk checks SharePoint: • Folder permissions correct • Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” • External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” Comparison internal vs external users: No visible difference. 08.04.25 08:45 Customer adds: One external user was newly added and immediately had access. Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically. 08.04.25 10:15 Helpdesk tests: • Remove + re-add to group → Access temporarily restored After approx. 1 hour, no access again. 08.04.25 14:30 Hypothesis: Token / cache / Conditional Access Test: User fully logs out → logs in again → Access remains denied Hypothesis rejected. 09.04.25 09:12 Helpdesk asks: Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”? 09.04.25 10:05 Customer: All externals are created as Guest. Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud. 09.04.25 11:40 First observation: For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute “source” = Windows Server AD For functioning external users: “source” = Azure AD Correlation unclear. 09.04.25 14:20 Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis. Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity 10.04.25 08:55 Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14 In-depth analysis 10.04.25 09:40 Azure AD Connect reviewed. Observation: Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud. On-Prem version is synchronized. Cloud version was originally created manually. Result: • External guests exist only in Cloud group • SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group Same name, but different Object IDs. 10.04.25 11:25 Review of group members via Graph: Cloud group: → Contains external guests Synchronized On-Prem group: → Contains only internal users Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect. 11.04.25 08:30 Reproduction in test: • On-Prem group re-synchronized • Cloud group overwritten • External users reproducibly lose access Root Cause Two groups existed with identical name: COMPLIANCE_TEAM 1. Cloud-native group 2. Synchronized On-Prem group Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated. SharePoint referenced the synchronized version. External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group. Actions 11.04.25 – 14.04.25 • Consolidation of group structure • Old Cloud group removed • On-Prem group properly cleaned • External guests managed as Cloud-only members • Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed 15.04.25 – Monitoring Access remains stable No further inconsistencies 17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 07 The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized). Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied. The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized. Access is now stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . 3) Desktop Outlook clients send stuck in Outbox (browser send works) - The matching incident showed Outlook desktop clients (legacy MSI build) could not authenticate to SMTP because tenant Security Defaults (or a tenant policy) disabled SMTP AUTH for legacy clients. Resolving it required selectively re enabling SMTP AUTH for affected users and adjusting Conditional Access policies; tests then succeeded
context: SOURCE 32: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox / sporadic SMTP error Ticket number: TI-1548823 Company: Customer Company 22 AG Contact person: Contact Person 08 Owner: Employee 34 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 14.01.25 08:17 Closure Closure date: 22.01.25 16:42 Closure text: Root cause identified (Hybrid Connector configuration). SMTP Auth adjusted, test successful. Ticket can be closed. Activities Emails: Created on: 14.01.25 08:17 Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22 From: Contact Person 08 To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company Hello everyone Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message: 0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed SMTP server not responding Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected. Internet connectivity is generally working. Could you please check this urgently? Kind regards Contact Person 08 14.01.25 08:21 Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08 Priority: High 14.01.25 08:29 Ticket assigned: Employee 34 Status changes 14.01.25 08:35 Status: In Progress Notes: 14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34 Contact established with Contact Person 08. Additional information: • Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11) • Webmail (OWA) works • Mobile devices (iOS) work • Error occurs only when sending • Receiving works Initial assessment: → Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue 14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting Actions performed: • Recreated Outlook profile • Removed and re-added account • Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS) • Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning • Checked client firewall Result: No improvement 14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis • Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring • No global Microsoft 365 outage reported • Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible • SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay 14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist). Reason: Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy 14.01.25 11:15 Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823 Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering 14.01.25 11:16 Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07 Further analysis (System Engineer) 15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07 Hybrid setup analysis: • Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365) • Send Connector checked • TLS certificate checked • SPF / DKIM unchanged Log analysis on Exchange Server: Event Log shows: 535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users. 15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified Tenant security policy was adjusted last week: Security Default → enabled Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI). 15.01.25 11:05 – Actions • SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users • Conditional Access Policy adjusted • Test sending successful 16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring Mailflow monitored for 24 hours No further errors in logs 21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback All users can send normally again. No further issues observed. Ticket closure 22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK Dear Contact Person 08 Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed. Root cause: SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy. Action taken: Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk

context: SOURCE 15: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF40: Customer Portal – Login via external URL not possible Ticket number: TI-1594408 Company: Customer Company 40 AG Contact person: Contact Person 19 Owner: Employee 29 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 03.06.25 07:52 Closure Closure date: 18.06.25 15:33 Closure text: Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to architectural specifications. Function restored. Activities 03.06.25 07:45 Phone report. Issue: External users can no longer log in to the customer portal. URL: https://portal.kf40.ch Browser error message: 502 – Bad Gateway Internally (via internal network) login works. Priority: High (customers affected) 03.06.25 07:52 Ticket created Assigned to: Employee 29 Status: In Progress Notes 03.06.25 08:30 Helpdesk checks: • External DNS resolution correct • Certificate valid • HTTPS reachable • Internal backend (app server) reachable Test: External → 502 Internal → works Suspicion: Reverse Proxy / WAF 03.06.25 09:10 Customer adds: A new certificate was installed over the weekend. Additionally, there was a firewall update (security patch). 03.06.25 10:25 Helpdesk checks firewall: • Rule for port 443 present • NAT correct • No blocking visible in logs Load balancer status: Backend server marked as “Healthy”. 03.06.25 11:40 Test with direct IP to Reverse Proxy → works. Test via FQDN → 502 Notable: Header “X-Forwarded-Proto” missing in external request. 04.06.25 09:05 Customer asks whether certificate may be incorrectly bound. Certificate validation: • CN correct • SAN correct • Chain complete No obvious errors. 05.06.25 08:50 Ticket forwarded to Network / Security Team. Assigned group changed to: BU Network & Security 06.06.25 09:30 Taken over by System Engineer 21 WAF analysis: • Policy active • TLS inspection active • HTTP/2 enabled Notable: After patch, default profile was reloaded. 06.06.25 11:15 Test: TLS inspection temporarily disabled → Login works. Inspection re-enabled → 502 again. 09.06.25 10:05 Further analysis: App server expects header: X-Forwarded-Proto: https When TLS inspection is active, header is not forwarded. 09.06.25 14:40 Comparison with architecture documentation performed. Reference in internal document: “Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2” Section 4.3 describes special header forwarding in case of TLS offloading. 10.06.25 08:25 Blueprint document loaded from DMS. Relevant notes: • TLS termination must occur on WAF • Backend expects HTTPS signaling • Custom header rewrite rule required • Inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers • Specific exception defined for portal application 10.06.25 11:45 Configuration comparison with blueprint shows: • Custom header rewrite rule missing • HTTP profile does not match documented version • Inspection template “Default-Strict” active instead of “Portal-App-Template” 12.06.25 Internal coordination: Firewall patch overwrote template. Custom policy was not reassigned. 16.06.25 Configuration reviewed and adjusted according to blueprint. No technical details documented in ticket (security policy). 18.06.25 14:55 External access test successful. Multiple login attempts tested. Monitoring stable for 24 hours. Ticket closure 18.06.25 15:33 Dear Contact Person 19 The root cause was a deviation in the Reverse Proxy configuration from the documented architectural specification following a security patch. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal blueprint documentation. The customer portal is now fully accessible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Closure description: Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment. 18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA Dear Employee 7, During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true: EnableJWT RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails. RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing. The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims. We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved. Kind regards Employee 21 Notes: 26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16. 26.11.24 Ticket assigned
